close_reason: Documented limitation: Vault KV v2 stores JSON maps, field extraction requires string values. Binary data should use base64 encoding.
description:
lib.rs:264-265: put() requires the SecretValue bytes to be valid UTF-8. This is correct for Vault KV v2 (which serializes data as JSON, so all values must be UTF-8 strings), but is not documented in the module-level docs or README.

Trigger: a caller with a binary secret (e.g. raw key material, certificate bytes) calls put with ?field= and gets DecodeFailed('secret value is not valid UTF-8').

Maintainer cost: 'why does my secret backend not store my secret' issues. The answer requires understanding the KV v2 wire format.

Fix: document the UTF-8 requirement in the module-level docs and README. Suggest base64 encoding for binary values. P3 because the error message itself is reasonably clear; the gap is just docs.
title: ?field= put requires UTF-8 — binary secrets cannot round-trip
